rationale: XiangShan Kunminghu V3 (OpenXiangShan, Mulan PSL v2) is the most mature permissively-licensed open RISC-V OoO RTL at flagship class and is selected as the e1-ultra big-core path. The big-core variant scales the same upstream RTL toward 8-wide decode / 512-entry ROB. No vendor IP license is required: the upstream commit is open and pinnable today. Tenstorrent Ascalon-D8 was surveyed as the leading commercial flagship-class core but rejected because its mobile-volume IP license terms are not published and procurement is not closeable on the program schedule.
fork_policy_reason: The big-core slot scales Kunminghu V3 from its native 6-wide config toward 8-wide / ROB 512. Per docs/architecture-optimization/sota-2028/ooo-execution.md Section F, the scale-up is gated on a microbench at 8-wide-issue before an RTL fork is cut; until then the upstream commit is pinned and integrated through a thin wrapper.

rejected_alternative.name: Tenstorrent Ascalon-D8
rejected_alternative.reason: no_published_mobile_volume_ip_license_terms; procurement not closeable on program schedule; closed commercial channel conflicts with open-chip charter

phone_class_claim_blocked_reason: Kunminghu silicon evidence exists only at academic nodes (per upstream XiangShan tapeout history). No 3 nm flagship PPA evidence and no 8-wide scale-up microbench yet. Selected as the open big-core path with a real upstream pin; flagship GB6/SPEC claims remain blocked until an 8-wide microbench and a 3 nm / N3P-class PPA report exist.
